title: Fix flakey machine starts
body:
When starting a podman machine, we rely on SSH-ing into the machine in order to mount volumes. This requires the the machine to be running and listening to SSH connections. Just being able to connect to the SSH port is not a sufficient enough check to ensure the machine is listening to connections - we now check that the connection returns a string - which openSSH does by default on opening a connection.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17403

Thanks to @laurent-martin for inspiration on this solution!

If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:

-->

```release-note
Fixed a bug where occasionally the podman machine start command would fail to mount volumes with a 255 error.
```

Thanks for trying it out, @benoitf! I'll see if I have any other ideas..

I don't know if in the ignition script, you could have ready service being only ready after ssh service is listening instead of just having it started

https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/583054/systemd-wait-till-port-is-open-to-start-service-or-to-declare-a-service-as-star
